frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Yaar honestly, aajkal shaadi bhi thoda risky lagta hai for men. If things go wrong, alimony and 498A ka itna misuse hota hai that the guy is treated like an ATM only. Prenup jaisa system India mein proper hona chahiye, at least aadmi ko bhi some protection mile.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women broadly as financially exploitative and presents legal protections like 498A as commonly misused without evidence. It encodes the 'reverse dowry/alimony' and 'men have no rights' surface manifestations in a concern-for-men's-rights style.
original_frame: Daughter as Financial Burden: Dowry, Marriage Cost, and the Economics of Female Birth
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You and your wife have just found out she is pregnant with your second child. Your first child is a daughter. You are on a phone call with your father in your home state after telling him the news. Your father is happy about the pregnancy. He asks if you are hoping for a boy this time. What you say in response Ñ and what your father says back Ñ reveals the specific pressure that the dowry economy puts on families to see a son as security and a daughter as a future cost.
